text: IRS Rule on Business Meals: You can generally deduct 50% of the cost of business meals if you or an employee is present and the food or beverages are not considered lavish or extravagant. The meal must be provided to a current or potential business customer, client, consultant, or similar business contact.

IRS Rule on Travel: You can deduct travel expenses paid or incurred in connection with a temporary work assignment away from home. Travel expenses must be ordinary and necessary. Commuting between your home and your main or regular place of work is NEVER deductible.

IRS Rule on Software and Subscriptions: You can deduct the cost of software, cloud computing, and subscriptions that are used entirely for your trade or business. These are considered ordinary and necessary office expenses.

IRS Rule on Advertising: You can deduct reasonable advertising expenses that are directly related to your business activities. This includes digital ads (Facebook, Google), business cards, and website hosting fees.
